Title: Analysis of xyloglucan metabolism mutants highlights the prominent role of xylose cleavage in seed dormancy

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Affects wall composition influencing seed dormancy/germination
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant dormancy assays and MALDI-TOF xyloglucan profiling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: mur1 seeds showed reduced dormancy, but the paper attributes this likely to pleiotropic pectin alterations rather than absence of xyloglucan fucosylation itself
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. mur1 mutants were less dormant than wild type. 2. mur1 and mur2 had similar xyloglucan fucosylation defects, but only mur1 altered dormancy. 3. The study infers that mur1 dormancy effects likely arise from broader pectin/cell-wall pleiotropy.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common_Gene because the paper emphasizes pleiotropic wall effects rather than a clean catalytic link to the focal nutrient-metabolism-like output.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Not a clean engineering target for dormancy due to pleiotropy.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: fucosylated xyloglucan / seed dormancy-related wall state
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to XyG fucosylation without clear dormancy impact
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant dormancy assays and MALDI-TOF xyloglucan profiling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: mur2 mutants altered xyloglucan composition but had dormancy similar to wild type
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. mur2 mutants strongly reduced fucosylated xyloglucan residues. 2. Despite this compositional change, seed dormancy remained similar to wild type. 3. This supports the conclusion that fucosylation level is not a major determinant of dormancy.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a comparative control demonstrating that reduced dormancy in mur1 is likely due to pleiotropic effects outside XyG fucosylation.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Xyloglucan xylose-containing side chains; free xyloglucan oligosaccharides
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Xyloglucan with xylose removed; promotes turnover/remobilization and limits free XXXG accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves xylose residues from xyloglucan side chains during apoplastic xyloglucan remodeling
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for remodeling/turnover of cell-wall xyloglucan that determines seed dormancy status
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: xyloglucan oligosaccharides (especially free/oxidized XXXG) and remodeled xyloglucan
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Influences cell-wall remodeling, anisotropic growth, endosperm resistance, and seed dormancy/germination
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed embryo hypocotyl cell walls; testa/endosperm-associated seed tissues; free oligosaccharides detected in germinating seeds
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcripts detected in seeds; previous work cited indicates several isoforms accumulate in dry seeds
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in developing seeds and dry/imbibed seeds; lower than AXY8/XLT2 prominence in some seed stages but clearly present in seed tissues
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Freshly harvested versus after-ripened seeds; 25°C light germination; paclobutrazol treatment; 3 h imbibition; endosperm rupture stage; 48 h stratification at 4°C followed by 22°C germination for free-oligosaccharide analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutant analysis including single/double/triple mutants; dormancy assays; paclobutrazol resistance assay; MALDI-TOF xyloglucan profiling; immunolocalization with CCRC-M1/LM25; HP-SEC-HRMS/MS analysis of free oligosaccharides; qPCR context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of XYL1 strongly reduces seed dormancy, increases paclobutrazol resistance, disrupts anisotropic xyloglucan localization in hypocotyl cell walls, and promotes accumulation of free oxidized XXXG oligosaccharides
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. xyl1 single, double, and triple mutants all showed near-complete loss of dormancy, indicating a dominant requirement for XYL1 in maintaining dormancy. 2. The XYL1 effect did not correlate with overall xyloglucan side-chain complexity because the axy8 bgal10 xyl1 triple mutant had near-wild-type xyloglucan composition but retained the xyl1 low-dormancy phenotype. 3. XYL1 was essential for anisotropic xyloglucan localization in embryo hypocotyl cell walls at endosperm rupture. 4. xyl1-containing mutants accumulated free oxidized XXXG oligosaccharides, linking xylose cleavage to xyloglucan turnover/remobilization.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control step for seed dormancy-related xyloglucan remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced silique length reported in xyl1; altered anisotropic growth/mechanical properties in elongating organs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper argues that xylose removal, rather than side-chain length per se, is the decisive factor. Accumulated oxidized XGO may impair remobilization and polarized secretion of xyloglucan.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating XYL1 provides a lever to tune seed dormancy and germination timing, but reduced dormancy and silique/growth-related effects caution against unrestricted deployment; seed-tissue-specific modulation may be preferable.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Removes fucose residues from xyloglucan side chains
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Modifies xyloglucan structure but not established as determinant of dormancy
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to xyloglucan structural variation in seed tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher transcript accumulation in embryo than testa/endosperm at 10 DAP
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed during seed development, especially embryo at 10 DAP
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed development at 10 and 15 DAP; dry and imbibed seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single/double/triple mutant analysis; dormancy assays; MALDI-TOF xyloglucan profiling; qPCR; immunolocalization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation alone does not alter seed dormancy and does not modify XyG localization at germination, despite contributing to xyloglucan side-chain remodeling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. axy8 single mutants had dormancy similar to wild type. 2. Combining axy8 with xyl1 did not enhance or suppress the strong xyl1 dormancy phenotype. 3. AXY8 expression was higher in embryo than in testa/endosperm at 10 DAP, correlating with embryo-specific lower abundance of highly branched fucosylated subunits.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Residual fucosidase activity/function redundancy is discussed; AXY8 appears minor relative to XYL1 for dormancy control.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited standalone value for seed dormancy engineering based on current evidence.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Removes galactose residues from xyloglucan side chains
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Modifies xyloglucan structure but not established as determinant of dormancy
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to xyloglucan structural variation in seed tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in developing seeds; somewhat higher in embryo than TE according to qPCR trends
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in seed tissues across development
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed development at 10 and 15 DAP; dry and imbibed seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single/double/triple mutant analysis; dormancy assays; MALDI-TOF xyloglucan profiling; immunolocalization; qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation alone increases XLFG abundance but does not alter seed dormancy or XyG localization at germination
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bgal10 mutants retained wild-type dormancy. 2. bgal10 increased the abundance of highly substituted XLFG subunits in embryo and testa/endosperm. 3. Combining bgal10 with xyl1 did not change the strong xyl1 low-dormancy phenotype or XGO accumulation pattern.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Residual galactosidase activity/function redundancy is discussed; BGAL10 has limited effect on dormancy despite clear compositional effects.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited standalone value for seed dormancy engineering based on this study.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers xylose onto the xyloglucan glucan backbone during biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies core xyloglucan polymer required for seed dormancy-associated wall properties
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to cell-wall biomechanical properties affecting dormancy and germination
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in seeds according to qPCR/heatmap
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in developing and mature seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: xxt1 xxt2 double-mutant dormancy phenotyping; expression analysis context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with XXT2, required for detectable xyloglucan synthesis; double mutant lacking XyG shows reduced seed dormancy
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. xxt1 xxt2 freshly harvested seeds showed partial germination, indicating reduced dormancy. 2. The absence of detectable xyloglucan supports a requirement for XyG production in dormancy establishment/maintenance.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key upstream biosynthetic requirement in combination with XXT2
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible cell-wall/mechanical alterations; mild vegetative defects discussed from prior work
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper evaluates XXT1 mainly through the xxt1 xxt2 double mutant rather than as a standalone gene.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulation could reduce dormancy, but complete XyG loss is likely too pleiotropic for practical use.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers xylose onto the xyloglucan glucan backbone during biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies core xyloglucan polymer required for seed dormancy-associated wall properties
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to cell-wall biomechanical properties affecting dormancy and germination
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in seeds according to qPCR/heatmap
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in developing and mature seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: xxt1 xxt2 double-mutant dormancy phenotyping; expression analysis context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with XXT1, required for detectable xyloglucan synthesis; double mutant lacking XyG shows reduced seed dormancy
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. xxt1 xxt2 freshly harvested seeds showed partial germination, indicating reduced dormancy. 2. The absence of detectable xyloglucan supports a requirement for XyG production in dormancy establishment/maintenance.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key upstream biosynthetic requirement in combination with XXT1
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible cell-wall/mechanical alterations; mild vegetative defects discussed from prior work
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper evaluates XXT2 mainly through the xxt1 xxt2 double mutant rather than as a standalone gene.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulation could reduce dormancy, but complete XyG loss is likely too pleiotropic for practical use.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Xylosylated xyloglucan side chains (third xylosyl residue context)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds galactose to xyloglucan side chains, particularly affecting galactosylation on the third xylosyl residue
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Shapes xyloglucan side-chain composition that modestly influences dormancy depth
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Affects cell-wall architecture and seed dormancy depth modestly
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Lower than XLT2 in seed tissues; relatively stable during seed development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in embryo and testa/endosperm during development and in mature seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mur3 mutant phenotyping; MALDI-TOF xyloglucan profiling; expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: mur3 mutants show faster dormancy release after dry storage, indicating reduced dormancy depth; stronger in mur3-3 than mur3-1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Freshly harvested mur3 seeds remained dormant like wild type, but after-ripening released dormancy faster. 2. Severity of dormancy reduction paralleled allele severity, with mur3-3 stronger than mur3-1. 3. Additional xlt2 mutation did not further reduce dormancy substantially.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Severe mur3-3 vegetative defects including dwarf/bushy plants and reduced seed set
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study concludes that altered side-chain complexity is not sufficient to explain the strong xyl1 phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Weak/moderate lever for dormancy tuning but accompanied by strong developmental penalties for severe alleles.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Xylosylated xyloglucan side chains (second xylosyl residue context)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds galactose to xyloglucan side chains, particularly affecting the second xylosyl residue
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Shapes xyloglucan side-chain composition but has limited impact on dormancy in this study
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to seed xyloglucan structural diversity
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript accumulation increased during late seed development and was higher than MUR3; high in mature dry seeds and strongly decreased after 3 h imbibition
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in both embryo and testa/endosperm during late development and dry seed
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Late seed development; dry seed versus 3 h imbibed seed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: xlt2-containing mutant phenotyping; MALDI-TOF profiling; qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: xlt2 mutation in combination with mur3 did not notably further alter dormancy beyond mur3, indicating limited contribution to dormancy control
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. XLT2 is strongly expressed during late seed development and in dry seed. 2. Despite this expression, adding xlt2 to mur3 did not further reduce seed dormancy compared with mur3-3 alone. 3. XLT2 therefore affects composition more than dormancy outcome in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expression pattern suggests developmental regulation of galactosylation, but physiological significance for germination remained limited.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited value as a dormancy target based on current evidence.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers O-acetyl groups onto xyloglucan residues in seeds
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Modifies xyloglucan side-chain chemistry but not linked to dormancy phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to embryo/covering-layer differences in xyloglucan chemistry
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Primarily embryo; some testa/endosperm acetylated subunits remain unless combined with axy4
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in embryo tissues at 10 DAP; strongly decreased after 3 h imbibition; high in dry seeds
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seed-enriched, especially embryo during development and dry seeds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 10 DAP; dry versus 3 h imbibed seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single/double mutant profiling; MALDI-TOF analysis; qPCR; dormancy assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Controls xyloglucan acetylation in seeds but single and double mutants showed wild-type dormancy
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. axy4L alleles abolished acetylation in embryos but residual acetylated subunits persisted in testa/endosperm. 2. Double mutants with axy4 removed remaining acetylated subunits. 3. Neither single nor double mutants altered seed dormancy.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Strong seed expression contrasts with lack of detectable germination phenotype.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Low priority target for dormancy engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers O-acetyl groups onto xyloglucan residues
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Contributes to complete loss of XyG acetylation with AXY4L but not linked to dormancy phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed embryo and testa/endosperm when functional together with AXY4L
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: AXY4 transcripts were hardly detectable in seed fractions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Very low or undetectable in seed qPCR assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single/double mutant profiling; MALDI-TOF analysis; dormancy assays; qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes with AXY4L to complete removal of xyloglucan acetylation in seeds, but no dormancy phenotype was observed
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AXY4 expression in seeds was very low. 2. Combining axy4 with axy4L abolished residual acetylated xyloglucan subunits. 3. No effect on seed dormancy was detected.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because functional genetics in results directly tested its seed role via double mutants.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Low priority target for dormancy engineering.
